disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Established, slowly progressive cognitive decline over about three years, on a regular memory tablet (donepezil)
trigger_question_examples: Has he been diagnosed with any memory or thinking problems before?
What is his usual level of day-to-day function?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He was diagnosed with dementia about three years ago and it has slowly got worse. He's on a memory tablet and needs help with everything day-to-day now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute change over roughly 48 hours — no sleep, relentless pacing and repeated attempts to leave the house, more agitated than ever before
trigger_question_examples: What has changed in the last day or two?
How is this different from his usual confusion?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: This started about two days ago — he just hasn't slept at all and won't stop pacing and trying to get out the door. He's never been this bad or this hard to settle.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New failure to recognise his wife of 40 years tonight, and a fixed belief he must go to work in the past
trigger_question_examples: Does he know who you are and where he is?
What is he saying or believing right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Tonight he doesn't know who I am, which he's never done before. He keeps insisting he has to get to work and that his boss is waiting.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Immediate safety risk — repeatedly trying to unlock the front door and dismantling barriers to leave the house at night, physically strong and mobile
trigger_question_examples: Is he trying to leave the house?
Are you able to keep him safe where you are?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He keeps trying to unlock the door and pull the chairs away that I've put in front of it. He's still strong and I can't physically stop him — I'm terrified he'll get out into the street.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Carer at the point of physical and emotional collapse — sole carer, two nights without sleep, states she cannot manage alone
trigger_question_examples: Is there anyone else there to help you tonight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm completely exhausted, I haven't slept either and it's just me here. I honestly can't keep doing this on my own tonight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Carer-reported absence of obvious infective or pain features — no obvious fever, cough, pain, or witnessed fall; taking some food and fluids
trigger_question_examples: Does he seem hot, or have a cough, or any pain?
Has he had a fall or hurt himself, and is he eating and drinking?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I don't think he's got a temperature and he's not coughing or complaining of pain. I didn't see him fall. He's had a little to eat and drink today.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't actually taken his temperature — I'm just going on how he seems.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Escalating agitation when contradicted or physically blocked from the door
trigger_question_examples: What happens when you try to stop him or correct him?
Is he becoming aggressive at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: When I tell him he doesn't have work or try to block the door he gets more wound up and starts pulling at the chairs. He's not hit me, but he's rougher than usual.
